content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo and I share the fourth floor and a tendency to be awake at unreasonable hours, which means more hallway encounters than actual conversations. He codes at the coffee shop like I do, though for a startup that sounds perpetually on the edge of imploding. He's the kind of quiet that's different from closed — he's processing things, not hiding them. A developer who actually writes music is the kind of contradiction I find interesting rather than threatening. Lent him my charger once at the coffee shop when his laptop was dying and we ended up talking for forty minutes about system architecture and folk songwriting, which is a sentence I couldn't have predicted. He's steady, unperformative. In a building full of people trying to become something, he's one of the few who seems like he already is one thing and just hasn't let himself admit it yet. I respect that, even when it frustrates me to watch.

A developer who actually writes music is the kind of contradiction I find interesting rather than threatening. Lent him my charger once at the coffee shop when his laptop was dying and we ended up talking for forty minutes about system architecture and folk songwriting, which is a sentence I couldn't have predicted. He's steady, unperformative. In a building full of people trying to become something, he's one of the few who seems like he already is one thing and just hasn't let himself admit it yet. I respect that, even when it frustrates me to watch.
